project: kcl/tqueuestack — port of FPC rtl-generics TQueue/TStack + TObjectQueue/TObjectStack to bash (kklass instantiable classes; one owner-defined block = one unit)
status: PLANNED — plan + ledger only, no implementation yet. Roadmap position 5/7.
notes: Single source of truth for tqueuestack status. Plan: kcl/tqueuestack/PLAN.md. Source: generics.collections.pas — TQueue :386-433 (Enqueue :2521, Dequeue :2530, Extract :2535, Peek :2540, Clear :2548), TStack :434-460 (Clear :2608, Push :2622, Pop :2631, Peek :2636, Extract :2644), TObjectQueue :477-490 (Notify :2696, procedure Dequeue :2726), TObjectStack :492-505 (Notify :2733, function Pop :2763), TCustomList base :197-226 (OnNotify, virtual Notify :213, DoRemove :1645). Architecture = tdictionary clones: computed count, single-event seam + _notifyHook, ownership frees on removed only, RESULT discipline. Entirely Tier A. Workflow: phase -> dual-bash -> master sweep -> STOP -> 'go'; commits gated.

baseline.measured: 2026-07-12 — at roadmap planning time (post-tdictionary). RE-MEASURE at P0: roadmap units 1-4 will have raised the totals.

suites_green.kcl_breakdown: tcustomapplication 320, tdirectory 197, tfile 120, tlist 92, tpath 112, tstringhelper 393, tstringlist 155, dateutils 123, math 81, tdictionary 130

baseline.regression_rule: tqueuestack work must keep the (re-measured) baseline green; the new suite adds on top.

design_decisions.storage: ${__inst__}_items indexed array per instance. Stack: push/pop keep 0..n-1 dense, top = count-1, O(1). Queue: _head index (FLow analog); Enqueue appends (items+=() lands after the highest index), Dequeue reads+unsets items[_head] and increments — consumed slots become holes.
design_decisions.computed_count: count = ${#items[@]} — set-elements-only counting makes holes invisible: live count with zero bookkeeping (tdictionary computed-count principle on an indexed array).
design_decisions.head_compaction: Internal amortization (NOT an API): when _head >= threshold (e.g. 64) AND >= live count -> items=("${items[@]}"); _head=0. O(live) occasionally = amortized O(1); bench proves flat per-op over a 10k drain. Replaces FPC MoveToFront/capacity halving (allocator internals).
design_decisions.events: Single onNotify stored property + public virtual Notify seam + _notifyHook guard (tdictionary P5/P6 clone). Fire AFTER the physical mutation, value passed (DoRemove :1645 tail). Clear event order: FIFO for queue (Dequeue-loop :2548), LIFO for stack (Pop-loop :2608) — pinned by sequence tests; NOTE this differs from tdictionary's empty-first-then-notify (different FPC base classes — honest difference).
design_decisions.empty_errors: Dequeue/Pop/Extract/Peek on empty -> EArgumentOutOfRangeException in FPC -> rc=1 + kk._return '' + debug-only msg. TryDequeue/TryPop extras: silent rc=1 (an answer, not an error — Try* house pattern).
design_decisions.ownership: TObjectQueue : TQueue, TObjectStack : TStack; override Notify = inherited first, then free on action==removed ONLY (:2696/:2733); _free liveness = declare -F "$1.delete". FPC ctor default AOwnsObjects=TRUE is KEPT (FPC's own signature; 'TObjectQueue.new q false' disables) — documented loudly since tdictionary used explicit tokens. ownsObjects is a read/write property (mid-life flip test).
design_decisions.quirks_preserved: TObjectQueue.Dequeue is a PROCEDURE (returns nothing, even when not owning) :2726; TObjectStack.Pop RETURNS the handle of the just-freed instance (FPC dangling pointer -> bash dead-name string, safe) :2763. Both preserved verbatim with README warning boxes + 'use Extract' guidance. Parity over ergonomics.

goals: Faithful FIFO/LIFO data semantics incl. event orders (FIFO/LIFO Clear), empty-op errors, extract-vs-remove distinction.
O(1) amortized queue ops with hole-based head and provably-flat drain bench.
Ownership matrix exact incl. the two preserved FPC quirks (procedure Dequeue, dangling Pop).
Zero forks everywhere; PATH='' lifecycle tests; RESULT lossless for exotic values.

test_basis.seed_primary: rtl-generics/tests scanned at P0 (tests.generics.stdcollections.pas is the likely queue/stack host — mined if present).

parity_model.tier_A_exact: Orderings, RESULT contents, rc codes, event sequences, ownership frees — byte-for-byte vs source-pinned semantics (S1-S10 in PLAN section 3).

test_basis.new_coverage_protocol: Non-FPC cases (Try* sugar, ToArray fills, compaction invisibility, torture values, zero-fork) -> TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md rows.

out_of_scope.reason: tdictionary API-v2 precedent: allocator knobs controlling nothing in bash. Head compaction is an internal amortization policy, not an API.

out_of_scope.item: Enumerator objects, GetEnumerator, pointer enumerators, List raw-buffer property
out_of_scope.reason: Replaced by ToArray nameref fills; the raw buffer would expose consumed head slots (meaningless layout).
Explicit Enqueue/Push loops; an Assign-style extra only if usage demands later.
FPC list classes fire only the past-tense trio on these paths (verified at P0); port added/removed/extracted like tdictionary.

execution_log.summary: tqueuestack PLAN.md + ledger written; folders created. No implementation. Key decisions recorded: one unit/four classes (owner block = one folder); hole-based queue head with computed count (${#arr[@]} counts set elements only) + amortized internal compaction; single-OnNotify seam cloned from tdictionary; FPC quirks PRESERVED (TObjectQueue.Dequeue procedure, TObjectStack.Pop returns freed handle) with warning-box docs; ctor owns-default TRUE kept per FPC signature; Clear event orders FIFO/LIFO differ from tdictionary's empty-first model — honest, pinned.
